pages.raw_text:
Editor Press: When l read the
letter the other day from the wo-
man who has a grievance because
she has 100000 worth of proper
ty and can't vote, l made up my
mind to write about my griev-
ance, which 1 believe will win
more votes for suffrage than hers,
because l am a victim of the most
unjust law that can affect woman,
the property law of Pennsylvania,
which gives a man the right to
will away all of the property, al
lowing the wife only a life interest,
or the income of one-third, even
where she earned most of it her
self.


Is Old, Poor and Blind.


am now a poor, blind, broken
old woman, depending on the char
ity of my widowed daughter, who
sews to support us both, and she.
too, is delicate from the effects of
that terrible law. l will have my
daughter write my story and you
can judge for yourself.


My husband and | started with
nothing. made vinegar. which
he peddled around in a wheelbar-
row. Besides making the vinegar
l kept boarders, did all the work,
washing, cooking, scrubbing, and
made all my husbands clothes, my
own and the children's.


In two years we had enough
money to start a little grocery. We
lived back of it, and l did all the
work, still keeping boarders, and
waited on the store all the time,
besides attending to all the bills,
as | had good head for business.
My husband didn't have much of
head for business.


In few years the business grew
so much, and | had so many chill
dren that we hired a girl to help.
But | worked always in the store,
because people seemed to want me
to wait on them. We finally be-
came rich and when my husband
died the estate was estimated at
million dollars, for we had the big
gest grocery business in the city
of Pittsburg, and lot of property
besides.


Grew Very Rich.


We had a fine home out in the
East End, but | went down to the
store every day in my carriage, for
| loved the store, and was proud of
the business we had made. And
everybody said it was Mrs.
who had built it up and was the
business one of the firm.


Well, my husband died in 1891
and left will that everything
should be sold and the money put
